title: SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST not rate-limited; burns Sapling diversifier indices and grows invoice table unboundedly
description: SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST has no rate limit (the check_rate_limit guard applies only to BROADCAST/SEALED_BROADCAST/WHISPER/GROUP_SEND). An authenticated user can call SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST at up to the connection's message rate, allocating a fresh Sapling diversifier index and creating a new invoice row on every call. The subscription_alloc_lock serializes allocations but does not prevent them. Consequences: (1) subscription_invoices table grows unboundedly per user; (2) the merchant wallet's diversifier index advances irreversibly through 2^88 at the attacker's pace; (3) purge_expired_invoices runs only every 100 blocks (~3 hours at 90s/block), so a burst attack accumulates thousands of rows before purge. Fix: apply check_rate_limit to SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST, or add a per-user cap on pending invoices before creating another.
